headlines.title: Watch live: 1,007-acre wildfire near Pleasanton prompts evacuation orders, 1 firefighter injured
headlines.description: Firefighters on Tuesday were battling a wildfire near Pleasanton that quickly spread to 1,007 acres and triggered evacuation orders. The Little Fire, which broke out near Vallecitos and Little Valley roads in the Sunol area, has triggered evacuation orders for the following zones: ALC-072, PLS-079, PLS-080, PLS-081, PLS-082 and PLS-103, according to Cal Fire. The blaze started at about 2:45 p.m. Crews were battling the fire with tankers and helicopters from the air and engines, crews and bulldozers on the ground. The fire was at 10% containment, with at least one unspecified firefighter injury, according to Cal Fire. Officers on the ground and in the air are assisting with evacuations in neighborhoods currently under evacuation orders, authorities said. Additionally, the department’s air support unit is actively fighting the blaze. The city of Pleasanton has opened an evacuation center at the Pleasanton Senior Center at 5353 Sunol Blvd. and requested assistance from American Red Cross shelter workers, the organization announced. The Red Cross said it will have two volunteers at the center tonight and is preparing additional staff for the coming days in case a full-time shelter is needed. Additionally, the city has county emergency trailers on site and can convert the facility into an overnight shelter if necessary, according to the Red Cross. The Alameda County Sheriff’s Office of Emergency Services stated temporary and emergency lodging resources are also available through the Tri-Valley program at http://bit.ly/4fwSzw8 The Alameda County Fire Department is assisting Cal Fire in the effort. The cause of the fire remains under investigation. Shadow Cliffs Regional Park is temporarily closed due to the fire, the East Bay Regional Park District Police Department announced. Message from state Sen. Jerry McNerney’s Facebook post reads as follows: “Evacuation orders are in place for the “Little Fire” burning in portions of the Tri-Valley. The fire has grown to approximately 950 acres this evening and is affecting communities in Sunol, Livermore and Pleasanton, including areas within the Pleasanton city limits. My staff and I are in contact with Pleasanton city officials, the Alameda County Office of Emergency Services, and CAL FIRE as they continue to respond. If you are under an evacuation order, please leave immediately and follow the directions of public safety officials. The evacuation center for Pleasanton residents is the Pleasanton Senior Center. Please stay safe, avoid the area to allow first responders to do their work, and continue to monitor official updates. Thank you to all of the firefighters, law and enforcement officers, and emergency personnel working to protect our communities. I will continue to post information on my accounts as new safety directives emerge.” This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: 1 dead after stabbing in parking lot of Fairfield business
headlines.description: One person died after being stabbed during an altercation in the parking lot of a Fairfield business late Tuesday morning, according to police. Police said they received multiple calls at about 10:50 a.m. about an altercation in the parking lot of a business along the 800 block of East Travis Boulevard. Witnesses told police that one person was stabbed during the altercation, police said. First responders transported the victim to the hospital, where they later died, according to police. The victim’s identity will be released following notification of next of kin. Additional information wasn’t immediately available. An investigation is underway.

headlines.title: 22-year-old man arrested for allegedly killing man in East San Jose
headlines.description: Editor’s note: An earlier version of this story identified the victim as female based on preliminary information from police. Authorities later said detectives determined the victim identified as male. This story has been updated. A 22-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of killing a man in East San Jose last week, police said Tuesday. Marco Jimenez, a San Jose resident, has since been booked into Santa Clara County Main Jail for murder in the killing of the unidentified man, who police say he knew. Officers first responded at about 12:59 a.m. Wednesday to a home on the 2100 block of Lanai Avenue to investigate a report of an unresponsive man, police said. Responding officers found the man unconscious and not breathing. Medical personnel tried to save him, but he died at the scene. At the time, the man had minor visible injuries, “but no obvious signs of fatal trauma were observed,” police said in a statement. Jimenez was detained at the scene and determined to be a person of interest, according to police. On Thursday, the medical examiner’s officer told detectives that the man’s manner of death was ruled as homicide, according to police. Detectives subsequently took Jimenez into custody. The motive and circumstances surrounding the homicide – San Jose’s 15th of 2026 – remain under investigation, police said. Anyone with information is urged to contact San Jose police Detective Sgt. Taylor or Detective Van Brande by email at 4257@sanjoseca.gov and/or 4542@sanjoseca.gov or by phone at 408-277-5283.

headlines.title: Gilroy Garlic Festival returns this weekend
headlines.description: The Gilroy Garlic Festival kicks off on Friday and runs until Sunday at the Hecker Pass Outdoor Events Center in Gilroy. Founded in 1979, the garlic festival has been a staple in California culinary culture, helping to showcase and celebrate the beloved cooking ingredient. In 2017, Guinness World Records certified the festival as the world’s largest garlic festival. “The Gilroy Garlic Festival has always been a celebration of both garlic and our local community,” said Alex Misa, president of the Gilroy Garlic Festival Association. In recent years, the festival has faced major challenges. In 2019, three people were killed in a mass shooting and the following year the festival was canceled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Since then, the festival has faced financial strains and was temporarily shuttered for a couple years before returning in 2025. The Gilroy Garlic Festival Association is a nonprofit organization that has awarded more than $12 million to community schools and service groups, and the entire festival is hosted by volunteers. Attendees should make sure to bring a breath mint this year as the festival offers a wide assortment of garlic entrees. At this year’s festival, people can enjoy garlic calamari, garlic scampi, pasta con pesto, pepper steak sandwiches, garlic bread, garlic fries, and gourmet all combo plates at the gourmet alley. For those brave enough, there’s also the festival’s famous garlic ice cream. Alongside the food there will also be live entertainment and cooking demonstrations. Guests can also make garlic braids and see a garlic topping demonstration. General admission is $35 and parking is $25. For tickets and more information on the Gilroy Garlic Festival, people can visit gilroygarlicfestival.com.
headlines.source: Lloyd B. Davis | Bay City News

headlines.title: Plans for first Costco in Oakland move forward
headlines.description: Oakland may finally be getting its own Costco. Mayor Barbara Lee said the city has entered into exclusive negotiations with the chain. The proposed Costco would take over the old Army base in West Oakland, just blocks away from Raimondi Park. The exclusive planning agreement is good for two years. If Costco does indeed move in, Lee said it will bring in $3 million in taxes each year for the city and create at least 400 jobs.

headlines.title: Palo Alto police arrest intruder found hiding in home bathroom
headlines.description: A San Jose man was arrested over the weekend after he entered a Palo Alto home and locked himself inside the bathroom while the homeowner was investigating outside the residence, police alleged. Officers responded at 2:36 a.m. Sunday to a home in the 3500 block of Julie Court after a resident reported a burglary in progress, according to the Palo Alto Police Department. Investigators said security cameras first captured the suspect on the property at about 1:15 a.m. About 45 minutes later, the suspect returned and rang the home’s doorbell. When the homeowner went outside to investigate, he discovered one of the family’s vehicles had been rummaged through. Police said the suspect then entered the home through the front door while the homeowner was outside. A female resident heard someone inside the house and called 911. Officers arrived within minutes, forced open a locked bathroom door and took the suspect into custody. Police identified the suspect as Oscar Dario Banegas, 46, of San Jose. He was booked into the Santa Clara County Main Jail on suspicion of felony residential burglary, felony parole violation, misdemeanor methamphetamine possession, misdemeanor resisting arrest, and misdemeanor providing false information to police after investigators said he gave officers a fake name. Police said nothing was reported stolen, and neither the residents nor the suspect was injured. Investigators said they are not aware of any recent similar cases in Palo Alto.

headlines.title: Dozens of devices donated to Antioch nonprofit after theft
headlines.description: An Antioch nonprofit that provides free tutoring to children is getting help after thieves stole dozens of devices just days before its summer program began. Last month, burglars broke into the RRTH Learning Center on Second Street and stole 40 Chromebooks worth about $20,000, according to the nonprofit. The devices were taken just days before the center’s free summer program was scheduled to begin. The program was started during the COVID-19 pandemic by Marjori and Sandro Trujillo. “We’re not going to let criminals deter from the progress that we’re making,” Sandro Trujillo said. On Tuesday morning, Amazon donated more than 50 Fire tablets and Kindles to help the nonprofit continue its work. The mayor of Antioch also joined the Trujillos to thank Amazon for its donation. NBC Bay Area’s Bob Redell has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Watch: Orcas launch out of Monterey Bay during dolphin hunt
headlines.description: Whale watchers in Monterey Bay witnessed orcas launching out of the water last weekend as they tailed a pod of dolphins. The owner of Sanctuary Cruises, which runs whale watching tours in Monterey Bay, said a boat captain witnessed the killer whales fly into the air between 10 to 15 times on Sunday. The orcas are natural predators and were feeding on the dolphins. The boat captain also spotted the orca pod on Monday.

headlines.title: Oakland City Council to vote on Coliseum complex sale
headlines.description: The Oakland City Council is expected to cast the final vote Tuesday on the sale of the Oakland Coliseum complex for $125 million. The city owns a 50% interest in the complex. Alameda County, which owns the remaining half, has already approved its portion of the deal. According to city documents, Oakland leaders are considering a second amendment to the original agreement that would provide better financial terms for the city. The deal would also allow for an initial $50 million all-cash sale of the Oakland Arena to the Oak View Group. Oak View Group co-founder Irving Azoff said the company plans to invest $40 million in upgrades to the arena.

headlines.title: San Francisco mayor launches childcare access online portal
headlines.description: San Francisco Mayor Daniel Lurie launched a new online portal for childcare and preschool access on Monday, designed to bring free and low-cost options to families. The Early Learning for All Parent Portal will be a website where parents can apply to preschools, identify financial assistance, and find childcare programs based on location or language, according to a news release from the mayor’s office. “Making childcare affordable lifts a huge burden for parents and strengthens the future of our entire city,” Lurie said in a statement. The release said providers have been and are in the process of joining the city’s ELFA network, bringing at least 30 new childcare providers onto the portal. Currently, the website contains more than 550, which is nearly 70% of the city’s licensed infant and toddler care programs. The ELFA network is an improved portal that will replace the city’s old portal, created nearly 10 years ago, the mayor’s office said. Earlier this year, families earning up to 150% of area median income, which is $243,150 for a family of four, could receive free care for an ELFA network program. As of July 1, families earning up to 200% of area median income, which is $324,200 for a family of four, could qualify for a 50% tuition subsidy for childcare programs. Additionally, the city’s investments will bring seven new childcare sites in fiscal year 2026-27, which will expand capacity for more than 250 children. “Expanding eligibility for low-cost childcare is a major step toward making San Francisco more affordable for families, but the benefit only works if families can find it, understand it, and use it,” said Ingrid Mezquita, San Francisco Department of Early Childhood executive director. “We looked closely at the parent experience and designed a new portal that makes it easier for families to access childcare.”

headlines.title: Jollibee sets opening date for downtown San Francisco restaurant
headlines.description: Fried chicken fans soon will have another option in downtown San Francisco. Jollibee is opening its highly anticipated restaurant near Union Square on Friday, July 31, making it the Filipino fast-food chain’s only location in the city. The opening date was first reported by the San Francisco Standard. The restaurant will be located on Market Street, next to the Powell Street BART station. According to SFGATE, the location has been in the works for six years.

headlines.title: Vallejo police investigate fatal shooting
headlines.description: Vallejo police are investigating a homicide after a person was shot Monday night. The shooting happened shortly before midnight outside a home on Lainey Court, just off Jack London Drive. Police have not released many details about the shooting. Video from the scene showed multiple bullet casings on the ground. According to an NBC Bay Area photographer at the scene, one person was shot and received CPR before being taken from the area. A Vallejo police spokesperson has since confirmed the shooting is being investigated as a homicide. No suspect is in custody. The victim’s identity has not been released. This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.
